title: cosmea pilosa
intro: Cosmea pilosa, also known as Mexican hair grass, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the family Compositae. The roots of Cosmea pilosa are used in traditional medicine, where they have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which may be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Cosmea pilosa have been associated with several health benefits, including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to aid in the management of diabetes and hypertension. The herbal preparation of Cosmea pilosa typically involves drying and processing the roots, which can be used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions for medicinal purposes.
